_meta.applies: every professional / community networking event (≈10–50 people), invariant. Venue/audience/optional content lives in separate modules.
_meta.note: Invariant networking core. The defining realities — solo arrivals, awkward first minutes, inclusion of newcomers, avoiding cliques, and follow-up — are treated as never-drop. Hired facilitator / AV / specific venue are optional modules.

phases.name: Foundations
phases.goal: Purpose, audience, size, budget, format, date, venue, needs

phases.name: Promotion & Invites
phases.goal: Registration open; invites sent; run-of-show, icebreaker & refreshments planned

phases.name: Preparation
phases.goal: Registrations confirmed; name tags & hosts ready; quantities finalized

phases.name: Final days
phases.goal: Final numbers; materials printed; hosts briefed

phases.name: Day-of
phases.goal: Set up, welcome, run networking, close

tasks.title: Define purpose & target audience (who should connect, and why)
Decide expected size / guest list (10–50)
Set budget target
Choose format (structured, free, or a blend) and event length
Set date & time (e.g. an after-work window)
Confirm a venue with good flow for mingling
Capture accessibility / dietary / language needs
Open registration capturing name, organization & interests
Promote & invite the target audience
Plan the run-of-show (welcome → icebreaker → networking → close)
Plan an icebreaker / structured introductions to ease the first minutes
Plan light refreshments & drinks
Track & confirm registrations
Prepare name tags (name + organization / interest)
Recruit & line up hosts/greeters/facilitators (welcome solo arrivals, fold in newcomers, prevent cliques)
Finalize headcount-based quantities (name tags, refreshments, space)
Confirm final numbers; print name tags & signage
Brief hosts on roles, the run-of-show, and the inclusion plan before doors
Set up the space for mingling + check-in / name-tag table + greeter
Welcome & open: set tone, purpose, format and simple ground rules
Run the icebreaker / facilitated introductions
Actively host: introduce people, fold in solo arrivals/newcomers, prevent cliques, manage structured↔free transitions
Close properly: clear wrap-up, thank-you, and a follow-up call-to-action / next step
Send follow-up: thank-you + recap/resources (and shared contacts if opted in)
Collect feedback for next time

critical_chains.note: No one left alone: line up hosts → brief them → actively fold in solo arrivals & prevent cliques
Networking's value is realized after: capture contacts → close with a next step → send follow-up
Beat the awkward first minutes: plan → open → run the icebreaker
Venue sizing needs purpose + expected size

risks.name: Attendees arrive alone and are left stranded
risks.mitigation: Greeter at check-in (NW-T19); hosts actively welcome and fold in solo arrivals (NW-T15/NW-T18/NW-T22); opening icebreaker (NW-T21).

risks.name: Awkward, slow start / no early momentum
risks.mitigation: Warm welcome + simple ground rules (NW-T20); planned icebreaker / structured intros (NW-T11/NW-T21); greeter from the door (NW-T19).

risks.name: Cliques form; newcomers excluded
risks.mitigation: Brief hosts to mix groups and include newcomers (NW-T15/NW-T18/NW-T22); structured introductions (NW-T11).

risks.name: Low or uncertain turnout / no-shows
risks.mitigation: Target the right audience and over-invite (NW-T09); reminders and confirmation (NW-T13).

risks.name: Budget overrun
risks.mitigation: Track spend vs target (NW-T03); venue and refreshments are the main levers.

risks.name: No follow-up — connections fizzle and value is lost
risks.mitigation: Capture contacts & interests at registration (NW-T08); close with a clear next step (NW-T23); send the follow-up recap (NW-T24).

risks.name: Poor venue flow / layout kills mingling
risks.mitigation: Choose a venue with good flow (NW-T06); set up the space for mingling, not seating (NW-T19).

decision_rules.rule: Default format: a short structured icebreaker, then free networking. Use pure free-form only for small (<15), familiar groups; add more structure when many first-timers or solo attendees are expected.
hosts_needed = ceil(attendee_count / attendees_per_host) (config 15); always staff at least one dedicated greeter at check-in.
Name tags are required when attendee_count > 12.
Recommend a 1.5–2h runtime; warn if the requested runtime exceeds 2.5h (energy fades).
Always capture name, organization and interests at registration to enable follow-up (activates CHAIN-FOLLOWUP).
Inclusion is never dropped: solo arrivals are greeted and introduced, and hosts actively prevent cliques (CHAIN-INCLUSION).
Honor accessibility / dietary / language needs captured at registration (NW-T07).

success_conditions.condition: Enough of the right people attended.
Attendees made at least one valuable connection.
No one was left alone; newcomers and solo arrivals were included.
Smooth start, clear transitions, on-time close.
Follow-up sent; connections have a clear way to continue.

failure_modes.mode: Attendees stand alone, disengage, and leave early
A slow, awkward start that never recovers
Cliques dominate and newcomers are shut out
Too few attendees to network meaningfully
No follow-up; connections fizzle and the value is lost
Poor layout; people get stuck and mingling dies
